title: Epsilon
summary: Epsilon is a medium-difficulty Linux machine that exposes a Git repository on the web server. During enumeration, leaked AWS Lambda credentials are discovered in Git commits, allowing the secret key to sign JWTs. With this key, admin access is obtained, SSTI is exploited for a reverse shell, and finally root is gained by abusing a cronjob running tar with the -h option via symbolic links.

title: Djinn3
summary: Djinn3 is a VulnHub Linux machine with multiple web services. Users enumerate and exploit misconfigured scripts to gain initial access, followed by privilege escalation via SUID binaries.

title: Joestar
summary: Joestar is a Windows-based VulnHub machine. Attackers perform web enumeration to find credentials, exploit remote code execution, and escalate privileges using misconfigured services.
